task_description: List and explain the three key assumptions underlying the Discovery method: proximity implies relevance, trend persistence beyond one-day wonders, and the 0.95 exponent optimality.
adaptation_notes: No code in this notebook. All assumption descriptions and their implications were LLM-drafted, then reviewed for alignment with Section 2 of the paper.
top_5_prompts: List the three key assumptions of the Goorha & Ungar Discovery method and explain each with its design implication.
Why does the Discovery system assume that proximity within 100 characters implies semantic relevance between two phrases?
Explain the assumption that genuine trends must persist beyond one-day wonders and how the 3-week baseline enforces this.
Why is the 0.95 exponent described as the best signal-to-noise trade-off rather than a theoretically derived value?
What are the limitations of assuming the 0.95 exponent is optimal when transferring the method to a different corpus?
